name: One, Two, Three, Four, Five (Chinese Pork Stew)
recipeIngredient: 1 pound pork spareribs, cut in half
1 tablespoon rice wine or dry sherry
2 tablespoons rice vinegar or white vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Use a sharp knife to cut between the spareribs.
2. Put the green onions at the bottom of a large saucepan.
3. Place the spareribs on top of the green onions.
4. Add the wine, vinegar, sugar, soy sauce, and water.
5. Cover and bring to a boil over high heat.
6. Reduce heat to low.
7. Cook for about 2 hours, then serve hot.
8. Makes about 4 servings.
Note: This recipe is really nice and good for weekends. I suggest serving it with white rice to soak up all the liquid. The name indicates how simple it is – it's from an old Chinese cookbook.
